opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

wattage.description: The total power output of the PSU in watts (e.g., 1000)

form_factor.description: The physical size and shape of the PSU (e.g., ATX)

efficiency_rating.description: The 80 PLUS efficiency certification level (e.g., 80 PLUS Gold)

cybernetics_efficiency_rating.description: The Cybernetics efficiency rating of the PSU (e.g., Cybenetics Titanium)

cybernetics_noise_rating.description: The Cybernetics noise rating of the PSU (e.g., Cybernetics A++)

modular.description: The modularity of the PSU cables (e.g., Full, Semi-Modular, Non-Modular)

color.description: The color(s) of this product. Each color of the product should be included in the array. Includes the casing color

length.description: The length of the PSU in mm (e.g., 140)

fanless.description: Whether the PSU operates without a fan

atx_24_pin.description: The number of ATX 24-pin (20+4) connectors (main motherboard power)

eps_8_pin.description: The number of EPS12V 8-pin (4+4) connectors (CPU power)

pcie_12vhpwr.description: The number of PCIe 5.0 12VHPWR (12+4) pin connectors (for high-end GPUs). Also referred to as 16-pin (12+4).

pcie_6_plus_2_pin.description: The number of PCIe 6+2-pin connectors (commonly used for GPUs)

sata.description: The number of SATA power connectors

molex_4_pin.description: The number of Molex 4-pin peripheral connectors (for older devices)

floppy_4_pin.description: The number of Floppy 4-pin connectors. Increasingly rare.

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

series.description: Product series name The series is the manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the PSU. Manufacturer (e.g., Corsair, Seasonic, be quiet!, EVGA, ASUS, MSI, Cooler Master, Thermaltake) is stored separately, as are wattage (`wattage`: 750, 850, 1000, 1200, 1500), form factor (`form_factor`: ATX, SFX, SFX-L), modularity (`modular`), efficiency rating (`efficiency_rating`: 80+ Gold/Platinum/Titanium), and length. Do NOT repeat any of those in the series. Wattage is NEVER part of the series. Examples: 'Corsair RM1000x 1000W 80+ Gold Fully Modular' → series='RMx'. 'Corsair RM1000x Shift 1000W' → series='RMx Shift'. 'Corsair HX1500i 1500W 80+ Platinum' → series='HXi'. 'Corsair AX1600i 1600W 80+ Titanium' → series='AXi'. 'Corsair SF1000L SFX-L 1000W 80+ Gold' → series='SF-L Series'. 'Seasonic Prime TX-1600 ATX 3.1 1600W 80+ Titanium' → series='Prime TX'. 'Seasonic Prime PX-1300 1300W 80+ Platinum' → series='Prime PX'. 'Seasonic Vertex GX-1000 1000W 80+ Gold' → series='Vertex GX'. 'Seasonic Focus GX-850 850W 80+ Gold' → series='Focus GX'. 'be quiet! Dark Power Pro 13 1300W' → series='Dark Power Pro 13'. 'be quiet! Pure Power 12 M 850W' → series='Pure Power 12 M'. 'EVGA Supernova G7 1000W 80+ Gold' → series='Supernova G7'. 'ASUS ROG Loki SFX-L 1000W Platinum' → series='ROG Loki SFX-L'. 'ASUS ROG Thor 1600W Titanium III' → series='ROG Thor'. The series is what stays consistent across wattage SKUs of the same product line.

variant.description: Specific variant in the series Most PSUs should have an empty variant. Wattage, efficiency rating, form factor, modularity, length, and color are all stored in their own fields. Only fill `variant` when the manufacturer ships a genuinely distinct sub-model under the same series + wattage that needs its own selector — for example a hardware revision marketed separately (v2, R2), an ATX spec refresh that was released as a distinct SKU under the same series name (e.g., 'ATX 3.0' → 'ATX 3.1' rev), or an OEM/regional spin. Examples: 'Corsair RM1000x 1000W' → variant=''. 'Corsair RM1000x 2024' (refresh marketed separately) → variant='2024'. 'Seasonic Prime TX-1600 ATX 3.1 1600W' → variant='ATX 3.1' only if both ATX 3.0 and ATX 3.1 SKUs of the same series + wattage are sold side by side; otherwise leave variant=''. 'be quiet! Dark Power Pro 13 1300W' → variant=''. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, wattage, manufacturer_color) group; if every PSU in a group ends up with the same value, leave it empty.

manufacturer_color.description: Manufacturer's official color name for this product variant. Use the manufacturer's marketed colorway / casing finish name verbatim. PSU colorways are usually simple: 'Black', 'White', 'Black / White', 'Silver', 'Matte Black'. For multi-tone PSUs, separate tones with ' / ' (e.g., 'Black / White'). Branded special / collab colorways DO belong here when the manufacturer markets them as a distinct colorway and the rest of the SKU is identical: 'Hatsune Miku Edition', 'Halo Edition'. Most stock PSUs are 'Black'. Do NOT put wattage ('1000W'), efficiency ('Gold', 'Titanium'), modularity ('Fully Modular'), form factor ('SFX', 'ATX'), or family / model words like 'RMx', 'HXi', 'Prime', 'Vertex' in manufacturer_color — those belong in dedicated fields or the series. Color is purely the visual finish of the PSU casing.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
